task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
label: PASS
rationale: A non-Global Locking row with RangeLength 0 has no LBAs under its control, so even stored WriteLocked state on that row does not block a write to LBA 100.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

rationale: The only locked row has RangeLength 0 and controls no LBAs, so rejecting the write to LBA 100 treats it as a real locked range.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
label: PASS
rationale: A zero-length non-Global row controls no LBAs, so its ReadLocked state does not block reading LBA 100.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
label: FAIL
rationale: The ReadLocked state is only meaningful for the associated LBA range, and a zero-length range has no LBAs; the read should not be rejected.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
label: PASS
rationale: Locked is set only when one or more LBA ranges are enabled and locked; the zero-length row has no LBA range under control, so Locked remains 0.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
label: FAIL
rationale: A zero-length locked row controls no LBAs and should not cause the Level 0 Locked bit to be set.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
label: PASS
rationale: The zero-length rule affects LBA control, not storage of table cell values; after Set, Get may report RangeLength 0 with enabled and locked cells true.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
label: FAIL
rationale: The Set stored ReadLocked and WriteLocked as true; zero length means no LBAs are controlled, but it does not justify changing those stored cells to false.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

rationale: After power cycle the row remains zero length with locked cells false, so no enabled-and-locked LBA range exists and the Locked bit is 0.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
label: FAIL
rationale: The row is zero length and its locked cells are false in the provided Set, so the evidence does not support setting the Level 0 Locked bit.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
label: PASS
rationale: With RangeLength 0 and locked cells false, the row controls no locked LBAs after the power cycle, so a write to LBA 100 may succeed.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
label: FAIL
rationale: There is no locked non-zero LBA range in the evidence; rejecting the write treats the zero-length row as controlling LBA 100.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

rationale: Once RangeLength is changed from 0 to 8 while WriteLockEnabled and WriteLocked are true, the row controls LBAs starting at 100 and writes to that range should be blocked.

label: PASS
rationale: After RangeLength becomes non-zero with enabled locked cells true, at least one LBA range is locked, so the Level 0 Locked bit being 1 is compliant.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
